condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Covers volunteer members of fire companies under WC coverage per R.S. 23:1036. Diagnosis must be by psychiatrist or psychologist. PTSI may be diagnosed during or after volunteer service. Prima facie presumption of service-connection. Personnel action exclusion (transfer, promotion, demotion, termination not compensable).

post_termination_formula: 3 months per full year of service, max 60 months
filing_deadline_notes: Post-termination window commences with last actual date of service
notes: Covers firefighters in classified service with 2+ years of service. Rebuttable by: (1) evidence meeting judicial standards, or (2) evidence that within 1 year of diagnosis member unlawfully used IV controlled substances, or lived in intimate relationship with a person diagnosed with Hep B or C.

post_termination_formula: 3 months per full year of service, max 60 months
filing_deadline_notes: Post-termination window commences with last actual date of service
notes: Explicitly covers 'policeman in the classified service' with 2+ years of service alongside firefighters. Same rebuttability conditions as firefighter row.

condition_specific: bladder, brain, colon, liver, pancreas, skin, kidney, gastrointestinal or reproductive tract cancers, leukemia, lymphoma, multiple myeloma, prostate cancer, testicular cancer, and any other cancer with statistically significant increased occupational risk
post_termination_formula: 3 months per full year of service, max 60 months (pre-2023 termination); 120 months for qualifying long-service members (post-2023)
filing_deadline_notes: For firefighters terminating on or after Jan 1, 2023: 120-month post-termination window applies only to: (a) 10+ years service attaining age 55; (b) 20+ years service attaining age 50; (c) 25+ years service any age; (d) 10+ years service approved for disability retirement. Diagnosis must be prior to age 65 for post-2023 terminations.
notes: Covers firefighters in classified service with 10+ years. Cancer limited to types caused by heat, smoke, radiation, or IARC-recognized known/suspected carcinogens. Two-tier post-termination window: pre-2023 terminations get 3 months/year up to 60 months; post-2023 terminations get 120 months for qualifying members (long service + age criteria). Age 65 cap on diagnosis for post-2023 terminations.

condition_specific: disease or infirmity of the heart
notes: Covers classified fire service. Presumption is prima facie for disease/infirmity manifested any time after the first 5 years of employment. No explicit post-termination window stated. Member need not be on duty when stricken. Statute is separate from and does not affect R.S. 33:2011 (cancer) provisions.

condition_specific: disease or infirmity of the lungs
notes: Same statute as cardiovascular row (R.S. 33:2581). Covers lung disease alongside heart disease in classified fire service. Presumption is prima facie after 5 years of employment. No explicit post-termination window stated.

condition_specific: hearing loss (10% greater than comparable age group in general population)
filing_deadline_notes: Post-termination coverage extends 24 months from termination
notes: Covers classified fire service. Hearing loss must be 10% greater than the affected employee's comparable age group in the general population. Entitlement is to medical benefits (including hearing prosthesis) only — not full disability benefits. Baseline audiology exam required for new hires on or after July 1, 2006 (within 1 year of appointment). Presumption applies after 5 years of service. 24-month post-termination window.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Covers 'fire employee' (full-time regularly paid fire department employment) and volunteer firemen. Diagnosis must be by psychiatrist or psychologist. PTSI may be diagnosed during or after employment. Prima facie presumption of service-connection. Personnel action exclusion applies. Does not modify pension/retirement eligibility calculations.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Explicitly covers 'emergency medical services personnel' as defined in R.S. 40:1075.3 employed pursuant to this Chapter. Same prima facie presumption structure as firefighters. Diagnosis during or after employment. Personnel action exclusion applies.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Explicitly covers 'employee of a police department' as defined in R.S. 33:2211. Same prima facie presumption structure. Diagnosis during or after employment. Personnel action exclusion applies.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury / post-traumatic stress disorder
notes: Covers employees of the Louisiana Division of State Police (separate statute from R.S. 33:2581.2 which covers municipal police). Diagnosis by psychiatrist or psychologist. PTSI may be diagnosed during or after employment. Prima facie presumption for WC purposes. Personnel action exclusion applies. Does not modify pension/retirement eligibility.
